prd.description: Implementar refresh token automático (7 dias) com recuperação em 401 + drag and drop no Kanban de tarefas usando dnd-kit

stories.title: Adicionar refresh token automático no NextAuth

Implementar reordenação dentro da mesma coluna
Adicionar indicador visual de drop zone

cd52a504.progressLog:
# Ralph Progress Log
Started: 2026-02-26T13:21:24.781Z

## Codebase Patterns
- NextAuth config is split into `auth.config.ts` (edge-compatible, no DB access) and `auth.ts` (with providers)
- Session config should be added to `auth.config.ts` since it's imported by both edge and node middleware
- Use `npx tsc --noEmit` for typecheck (no typecheck script in apps/web package.json)
- Project uses npm (packageManager field in root package.json)
- dnd-kit packages are already installed: @dnd-kit/core ^6.3.1, @dnd-kit/sortable ^10.0.0, @dnd-kit/utilities ^3.2.2

---

## 2026-02-26 14:10 - US-008
- Verified all acceptance criteria already implemented in previous iterations:
  - Placeholder shows exact position ✅ (h-2 bg-nexus-accent/30 border-dashed)
  - Different style for valid/invalid zones ✅ (nexus-accent colors)
  - Immediate visual feedback ✅ (CSS transitions)
  - Cursor changes to grabbing ✅ (cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing)
- No code changes needed - all features already present
- Typecheck passes
- **Learnings:**
  - Sometimes acceptance criteria span multiple stories
  - Verify existing code before implementing duplicates
  - Kanban board now fully supports drag & drop with visual feedback
---

## 2026-02-26 14:05 - US-007
- Implemented within-column reordering in kanban-board.tsx
- Enhanced handleDragEnd to detect if dropped on another task
- When same column: calculates new order based on target task position
- Uses getColumnTasks to get column tasks for index calculation
- Calls onUpdateTask with new order value
- Visual improvements:
  - Added drop indicator placeholder (h-2 bg-nexus-accent/30 border-dashed)
  - Shows above target task when dragging over
  - Task being dragged has reduced opacity (0.5) and scale effect
  - GripVertical indicator appears on hover
  - Smooth CSS transitions via transition-all
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Fragment `<>...</>` must have closing tag or all JSX breaks
  - CSS transform/transition from dnd-kit utilities for smooth animations
  - SortableContext with verticalListSortingStrategy for within-column sorting
  - Check if over.id is a task ID (not column status) to detect within-column drops
---

## 2026-02-26 13:55 - US-006
- Rewrote kanban-board.tsx with full dnd-kit integration
- Wrapped board with DndContext from @dnd-kit/core
- Used closestCorners for collision detection
- Each column is a Droppable using useDroppable hook
- Each task uses useSortable for drag functionality
- SortableContext with verticalListSortingStrategy for column tasks
- On drag end, calls onUpdateTask with new status
- Visual feedback:
  - Dragged task has reduced opacity (0.5)
  - DragOverlay shows preview during drag
  - Column highlighted with bg-nexus-accent/10 when dragging over
  - GripVertical indicator appears on hover
- Files changed:
  - `apps/web/src/components/kanban-board.tsx` - Complete rewrite with dnd-kit
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - @dnd-kit/core provides: DndContext, useDroppable, DragOverlay, closestCorners
  - @dnd-kit/sortable provides: SortableContext, useSortable, verticalListSortingStrategy
  - useSortable returns: attributes, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition
  - CSS.Transform.toString(transform) for applying transform styles
  - activationConstraint: { distance: 8 } prevents accidental drags
  - closestCorners is better for Kanban than rectIntersection
  - DragOverlay must be a direct child of DndContext, not inside columns
---

## 2026-02-26 13:48 - US-005
- Verified dnd-kit packages are already installed in the project
- Packages present: @dnd-kit/core ^6.3.1, @dnd-kit/sortable ^10.0.0, @dnd-kit/utilities ^3.2.2
- No code changes needed - packages already available
- Typecheck passes
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Always check package.json before attempting to install packages
  - The project uses npm (not pnpm) due to packageManager field in root package.json
  - dnd-kit v10 (sortable) has a different API structure than earlier versions
---

## 2026-02-26 13:42 - US-004
- Updated updateTask() to accept optional 'order' and 'newOrder' parameters
- When status changes, order is calculated to place at end of new column via getNextOrderForStatus()
- Added recalculateOrderForColumn() helper for within-column reordering
- Uses db.transaction() for race condition safety
- Updated Task type to include optional 'order' field
- Files changed:
  - `apps/web/src/actions/task-actions.ts` - Updated updateTask and added helpers
  - `apps/web/src/types/index.ts` - Added order field to Task interface
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Drizzle transactions use `db.transaction(async (tx) => {...})`
  - Use `tx` instead of `db` inside transactions for consistency
  - COALESCE(MAX(order), -1) pattern handles empty columns correctly
  - TaskStatus type needs to be cast from data.status when used in queries
---

## 2026-02-26 13:35 - US-003
- Added 'order' integer field to tasks schema with default value 0
- Added composite index on (status, order) for efficient Kanban ordering
- Created manual migration file since drizzle-kit generate failed due to pre-existing corrupted meta files
- Files changed:
  - `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts` - Added order field and index
  - `apps/web/drizzle/0010_task_order_field.sql` - Manual migration SQL
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Drizzle ORM uses `index()` function from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core' for composite indexes
  - The callback syntax for pgTable: `(table) => ({ idxName: index('name').on(table.col1, table.col2) })`
  - Pre-existing drizzle meta files are corrupted - `drizzle-kit generate` doesn't work
  - Manual migrations can be created in `apps/web/drizzle/` directory
  - Use `IF NOT EXISTS` in migration SQL for idempotent migrations
---

## 2026-02-26 13:30 - US-002
- Created authFetch utility with 401 error recovery
- On 401, attempts session refresh via /api/auth/session endpoint
- Retries original request if session refresh succeeds
- Redirects to login with callbackUrl preserving current URL if refresh fails
- Added helper methods: authGet, authPost, authPut, authDelete for convenience
- Files changed:
  - `apps/web/src/lib/auth-fetch.ts` - New file with fetch interceptor
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - NextAuth v5 uses `/api/auth/session` endpoint for client-side session checks
  - Client-side session refresh requires calling the session endpoint, not `auth()` (server-only)
  - The `credentials: 'include'` option is important for cookie-based sessions
  - This utility is opt-in - existing fetch calls continue to work, new code can use authFetch
---

## 2026-02-26 13:25 - US-001
- Implemented automatic refresh token in NextAuth configuration
- Added session.maxAge for 7 days (604800 seconds)
- Added JWT refresh logic when token is within 1 day of expiry
- Token age tracked via `createdAt` timestamp in JWT token
- Files changed:
  - `apps/web/src/auth.config.ts` - Added session config and JWT refresh logic
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - NextAuth v5 uses `session.strategy: 'jwt'` by default for Credentials provider
  - The `jwt` callback receives `trigger` parameter that can be 'update' for session updates
  - JWT token age tracking requires storing timestamp in the token itself
  - Pre-existing lint errors in vault-actions.ts and other files are not blocking
---
